notes: Best round to date by PnL, win rate, and drawdown. R3.A YES-edge filter validated. No new lever justified by data.

User-authorized mid-round Kelly bump. Trigger: R4 cumulativePnlUsd > $11.23 at 02:30Z. Cost: R4 becomes mixed-Kelly round (first 4-5 trades at $50, remainder at $250). R5 will need to validate $250 at full n=30.

verdict: CATASTROPHIC. Mid-round Kelly bump from $50 to $250 was textbook over-betting. R3's measured 70% win rate did not generalize; R4 saw a regime where 9 of last 10 trades lost. Hard-stop mechanism worked but overshot by $3.59 due to concurrent in-flight orders settling after stop trigger.
lessons: Never bump Kelly more than 2x in a single step
Mid-round bump is methodologically broken — destroys round-as-validation
Hard-stop is reactive (settle-time), not predictive; high-Kelly concurrent orders can overshoot
Yellow-signal cadence (1hr) is too slow for high-Kelly regimes; needs 15-min check
R3's +$9.48 / 70% win rate at n=30 was insufficient evidence for 5x scale-up

state_action: cumulativePnlUsd RESET 0; tradesSubmittedTotal RESET 0; candidates audit preserved
notes: User-authorized R5 launch at $50 Kelly (reverting from $250 R4 catastrophe). cumPnL reset to 0 to give R5 a clean hard-stop budget. Total session PnL across R1-R4 was -$18.59; tracked here, not in state.cumPnL.

reason: Statistical signal conclusive: R5 win rate at n=24 was 33% vs R3 at n=30 was 70%. Fisher exact p<=0.001. Remaining 5 trades not informative; expected to add -$1.50 to -$2 loss for no incremental evidence value.
verdict: REGIME-DEPENDENT OR FLUKE. R3-identical config produced opposite outcome. Two hypotheses survive: (a) R3 was a regime-specific edge that fails outside the US-evening window; (b) R3 was a statistical outlier. Cannot distinguish without testing R6 in same time window as R3 (18:00-04:00Z).

decision_criteria.regime_confirmed: R6 final PnL >= + (R3-shaped result with same config)

notes: User overrode 19:21Z exact-match for 18:05Z immediate launch. Still inside R3-equivalent US-evening window. State reset to cumPnL=0, tradesSubmittedTotal=0; in-flight R5 trade will reconcile on worker hydration, accounted as R6 PnL (small known bias).

reason: Worker filtering ~100% of candidates in final ~90 min (σ explosion + low edges); pace dropped to 0/hour from 4-6/hour. Called the round at n=28 instead of waiting indefinitely for 2 more fills.
key_insight: R3 and R6 not statistically different (Fisher p=0.37). R3 was real but ~2x variance peak above true expectancy. Aggregate strategy edge is -4.54c/contract, below +2.5c Week-4 gate. Validator A/B BRTI 168 vs Binance 20 is decisive (p=1.27e-30).
r7_recommendation: Option C (stop live, build OFI/basis features in shadow mode) per Brier analysis. Option A (time-gate 18-04Z + BTC blacklist) or B (tight 20-00Z window) are valid if continuing live with $18 bankroll remaining.
